focus: Emphatic & Contrastive Stress in Extended Argumentation
focus_ar: النبر التوضيحي والتناقضي في الحجج الموسعة
arabic_challenge: Week 4 introduced basic contrastive stress on single words. This week extends that skill into full, extended arguments — the kind needed for L2's debate and analysis missions on complex topics (politics, ethics, society). Native speakers strategically stress key argument words ACROSS multiple sentences to build a persuasive throughline, not just within one isolated sentence. Arabic-accented English sometimes applies emphasis inconsistently across a longer argument, weakening its persuasive impact even when the logic is sound.

target_sounds: building emphasis across multiple connected sentences

isolation.instructions: In an extended argument, choose 2-3 KEY words across your whole argument (not just one sentence) and consistently emphasize them each time they reappear, creating a persuasive thread.
isolation.instructions_ar: في حجة موسعة، اختار ٢-٣ كلمات أساسية في كل الحجة (مش بس جملة واحدة) وأكد عليها بثبات كل ما تتكرر، عشان تعمل خيط إقناعي متصل.
practice: EVIDENCE shows this works. This EVIDENCE is clear. We cannot ignore this EVIDENCE. (consistent emphasis on 'evidence' throughout)

sentence_practice: The EVIDENCE shows this policy works.
This EVIDENCE is clear and undeniable.
We cannot ignore this EVIDENCE any longer.
daily_drills.record_this: The EVIDENCE shows this policy works. This EVIDENCE is clear and undeniable. We cannot ignore this EVIDENCE any longer.
daily_drills.self_check: Did you consistently emphasize your chosen key word every time it appeared, building a clear persuasive thread?

target_sounds: contrastive stress to dismantle a counter-argument

isolation.instructions: When addressing an opposing view, native speakers often stress the CONTRASTING word heavily to highlight exactly where they disagree.
isolation.instructions_ar: لما تتعامل مع رأي معارض، المتكلم الأصلي غالبًا بيأكد بقوة على الكلمة المتناقضة عشان يوضح بالضبط فين نقطة الاختلاف.
practice: Some say it's EXPENSIVE. But it's actually an INVESTMENT. (contrast between 'expensive' and 'investment')

pair: it's EXPENSIVE... it's an INVESTMENT (clear contrast)

sentence_practice: Some say it's EXPENSIVE, but it's actually an INVESTMENT.
This is NOT a cost — it's an OPPORTUNITY.
People call it a RISK, but I call it a CHANCE.
daily_drills.record_this: Some say it's EXPENSIVE, but it's actually an INVESTMENT — this is NOT a cost, it's an OPPORTUNITY, and people call it a RISK, but I call it a CHANCE.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the contrasting pairs (expensive/investment, cost/opportunity, risk/chance) stand out clearly against each other?

target_sounds: escalating emphasis for a climactic conclusion

isolation.instructions: Strong arguments often build intensity toward the final, most important point — practice gradually increasing your vocal emphasis and pace toward a strong concluding statement.
isolation.instructions_ar: الحجج القوية غالبًا بتبني كثافة نحو النقطة الأخيرة الأهم — تدرب على زيادة تدريجية في التأكيد الصوتي والسرعة نحو تصريح ختامي قوي.
practice: This matters. This REALLY matters. This is why we MUST act NOW. (escalating intensity)

sentence_practice: This is why we MUST act NOW.
daily_drills.record_this: This matters. This REALLY matters. This is why we MUST act NOW — there is no time left to wait.
daily_drills.self_check: Did your intensity genuinely build sentence by sentence, landing hardest on the final call to action?

target_sounds: emphasis combined with thought-group pausing (integration with Week 8)

isolation.instructions: Combine this week's emphasis skills with last week's thought-group pausing — a well-placed pause right before your most emphasized word makes it land even harder.
isolation.instructions_ar: دمج مهارات التأكيد هذا الأسبوع مع وقفات مجموعات الفكر من الأسبوع اللي فات — وقفة موضوعة بعناية قبل كلمتك الأكثر تأكيدًا بتخليها تأثر أكتر.
practice: And the result? | ABSOLUTE failure. (pause before the emphasized word)

sentence_practice: What we need is | REAL change.
daily_drills.record_this: And the result? ABSOLUTE failure. The answer is EXACTLY right — what we need now is REAL change.
daily_drills.self_check: Did the pause right before your key word make the emphasis land with more impact?

target_sounds: full extended argument with integrated emphasis techniques

isolation.instructions: Deliver a spontaneous 60-second persuasive argument on a topic of your choice, integrating consistent key-word emphasis, contrastive stress, escalating intensity, and strategic pausing.
isolation.instructions_ar: قدم حجة إقناعية عفوية لمدة ٦٠ ثانية عن موضوع من اختيارك، بدمج التأكيد المتسق على الكلمة الأساسية، والنبر التناقضي، والكثافة المتصاعدة، والتوقف الاستراتيجي.
practice: Choose a simple persuasive topic and argue for it using all 4 techniques from this week.

daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: deliver a 60-second persuasive argument, using consistent emphasis, contrast, escalation, and pausing.)
daily_drills.self_check: Did all 4 techniques appear naturally in your spontaneous argument, working together persuasively?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: consistent key-word emphasis, contrastive stress, escalating intensity, pause+emphasis combination
challenge_sentences: The EVIDENCE is clear. Some call it EXPENSIVE, but I call it an INVESTMENT — and the result? | REAL, lasting change.
This matters. This REALLY matters. The EVIDENCE proves it, and the answer is | EXACTLY what we need.
People call it a RISK, but I call it an OPPORTUNITY — this EVIDENCE is why we MUST act NOW.
daily_drills.record_this: The EVIDENCE is clear. Some call it EXPENSIVE, but I call it an INVESTMENT — and the result? REAL, lasting change. This matters. This REALLY matters.
daily_drills.self_check: This passage combines all 4 techniques. Did each one come through distinctly and persuasively?

test_yourself.instructions: Record a 60-90 second persuasive speech defending a position on a topic that matters to you, integrating all of this week's emphasis techniques naturally.
test_yourself.passage: (Free speech: choose a real topic you care about and deliver a persuasive 60-90 second argument, using consistent key-word emphasis, contrastive stress against a counter-argument, escalating intensity, and strategic pause+emphasis for your conclusion.)

sounds_to_check.consistent key-word emphasis: check throughout for a repeated, emphasized key term
sounds_to_check.contrastive stress: check for at least one clear contrast against an opposing view
sounds_to_check.escalating intensity: check if the delivery builds toward the conclusion

scoring_guide.90_100: All 4 techniques used naturally and effectively, producing a genuinely persuasive-sounding argument.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most techniques present; delivery is fairly persuasive with minor gaps.
scoring_guide.50_69: Techniques present but inconsistent; argument sounds informative rather than persuasive.
scoring_guide.below_50: Flat, unemphasized delivery throughout — go back and repeat Days 1-4, deliberately exaggerating each technique.

for_arabic_speakers: This week is the culmination of L2's intonation/stress work (Weeks 4, 6, 8, 9) — it's where all those individual skills combine into genuinely persuasive, native-sounding argumentation.
This directly supports the blueprint's Table 8 'Debate Club' voice lounge sessions and L2's frequent debate-style speaking missions.

Native speakers vary these techniques by context — a courtroom lawyer, a political speech, and a casual argument with a friend all use different intensities of the same underlying tools. Practice matching your intensity to your actual context.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 and Day 7's spontaneous persuasive speech — these are strong candidates for peer feedback in your Level 2 debate voice lounge.
